cards.front: What is the purpose of a sliding window protocol in the data link layer?
cards.back: To allow for efficient data transfer by specifying the amount of data that can be sent in a single transmission

cards.front: How does a sliding window protocol work?
cards.back: One device sends data to another and both devices agree on the size of the data window, the device sending data sends the first packet of data within the window size

cards.front: What is the maximum window size?
cards.back: The maximum window size is typically set to a value between 1 and 64KB, depending on the network requirements

cards.front: What happens when the receiving device has received all the data within the window size?
cards.back: The receiving device sends an acknowledgement (ACK) to the sender, indicating that it has received all the data within the window size

cards.front: What is the function of the window's maximum size?
cards.back: The window's maximum size helps prevent data loss by ensuring that the receiving device receives all the data before sending an ACK, reducing the need for retransmissions
